_meta.notes: severity: Low | Medium | Medium-High | High | Critical. prevalence: Rare | Low | Medium | Medium-High | High | Universal

common_issues.name: Hip Dysplasia
common_issues.early_warning: Stiffness when rising, bunny hopping gait

common_issues.name: Cancer (Hemangiosarcoma)
common_issues.early_warning: Sudden lethargy, pale gums, abdominal swelling

common_issues.name: Elbow Dysplasia
common_issues.early_warning: Front leg lameness, reluctance to exercise

common_issues.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy
common_issues.early_warning: Night blindness, hesitancy in dim light

profiles.care_tips: Annual screenings for cancer are highly recommended after age 5. OFA hip and elbow certifications are key when purchasing a puppy.

common_issues.name: Brachycephalic Syndrome
common_issues.early_warning: Excessive panting, noisy breathing, exercise intolerance

common_issues.name: Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD)
common_issues.early_warning: Reluctance to jump, back pain, hind limb weakness

common_issues.name: Skin Fold Dermatitis
common_issues.early_warning: Redness or odor in facial or tail folds

profiles.care_tips: Avoid heat and strenuous exercise. Maintain a healthy weight to protect the spine. Clean skin folds weekly to prevent infections.

common_issues.name: Obesity
common_issues.early_warning: Weight gain, reduced activity, difficulty breathing

common_issues.name: Exercise-Induced Collapse
common_issues.early_warning: Weakness or collapse after intense exercise

profiles.care_tips: Monitor diet carefully — Labs are highly food motivated and prone to obesity. Regular low-impact exercise helps protect joints.

common_issues.name: Degenerative Myelopathy
common_issues.early_warning: Hind limb weakness or dragging, loss of coordination

common_issues.name: Exocrine Pancreatic Insufficiency
common_issues.early_warning: Weight loss despite good appetite, loose stools

profiles.care_tips: OFA hip testing is essential. Feed multiple small meals to reduce bloat risk. Watch for rear-end weakness in dogs over 7.

common_issues.name: Brachycephalic Syndrome
common_issues.early_warning: Loud snoring, labored breathing, blue-tinged gums

common_issues.name: Cherry Eye
common_issues.early_warning: Red bulge in corner of eye

common_issues.name: Skin Fold Infections
common_issues.early_warning: Redness, odor, or discharge in skin folds

profiles.care_tips: Keep cool at all times — bulldogs cannot pant efficiently. Clean all skin folds daily in hot weather. Limit exercise in heat.
Feed from an elevated bowl and split meals to reduce bloat risk. Annual thyroid and Addison's screening recommended for adults.

common_issues.name: Patellar Luxation
common_issues.early_warning: Skipping or hopping gait, occasional leg-lifting

common_issues.name: Dental Disease
common_issues.early_warning: Bad breath, reluctance to eat hard food

common_issues.name: Hydrocephalus
common_issues.early_warning: Dome-shaped skull, seizures, behavioral changes in puppies

profiles.care_tips: Use a harness instead of a collar to protect the trachea. Daily tooth brushing is essential — small mouths mean dense teeth.

common_issues.name: Zinc-Responsive Dermatosis
common_issues.early_warning: Crusty or scaly skin around face or paws

profiles.care_tips: Annual eye exams (CAER) are strongly recommended. High-exercise needs — under-exercised Huskies develop destructive behaviors.
Prophylactic gastropexy is strongly recommended to prevent fatal bloat. Annual cardiac screening after age 3. Lifespan 7–10 years — plan for high medical costs.

common_issues.name: Epilepsy
common_issues.early_warning: Seizures, temporary disorientation, loss of consciousness

common_issues.name: Cherry Eye
common_issues.early_warning: Red bulge in inner corner of eye

profiles.care_tips: Beagles are prone to obesity due to food motivation — measure meals carefully. Ear infections are common; clean weekly.
Harness only — no collars. Daily dental care is critical. Small Yorkshire Terrier puppies need frequent small meals to prevent blood sugar drops.

common_issues.name: MDR1 Gene Mutation
common_issues.early_warning: Adverse drug reactions; test before medications

profiles.care_tips: MDR1 (ABCB1) gene test before any medication. CEA eye test for puppies. Border Collies need 2+ hours exercise daily — mental stimulation equally critical.

common_issues.name: Mitral Valve Disease
common_issues.early_warning: Heart murmur (detected by vet), coughing, exercise intolerance

common_issues.name: Syringomyelia (SM)
common_issues.early_warning: Scratching at neck/shoulders without contact, vocalizing in pain

common_issues.name: Episodic Falling Syndrome
common_issues.early_warning: Sudden freezing or falling during exercise or excitement

profiles.care_tips: Annual cardiac screening by a cardiologist is essential from age 1. CKCS should not be bred before 5 years old and a clear cardiac exam (MVD protocol). Budget for significant cardiac care in later life.

common_issues.name: Hip & Elbow Dysplasia
common_issues.early_warning: Limping, difficulty rising, reluctance to exercise

common_issues.name: Osteosarcoma
common_issues.early_warning: Sudden lameness, swelling at limb joint

profiles.care_tips: OFA certifications for hips and elbows are essential when purchasing. Prophylactic gastropexy is recommended. Annual cardiac screening. High insurance costs are justified for this breed.

common_issues.name: Boxer Cardiomyopathy (ARVC)
common_issues.early_warning: Sudden collapse, arrhythmia detected by Holter monitor

profiles.care_tips: Annual cardiac evaluations and Holter monitoring are strongly recommended after age 3. Avoid heat — Boxers have reduced heat tolerance. Budget high for cardiac care.

common_issues.name: MDR1 Gene Mutation
common_issues.early_warning: Drug sensitivity — test before any medications

profiles.care_tips: MDR1 gene test is essential before any medication. CEA screening for puppies. Aussies need significant daily exercise and mental stimulation to prevent behavioral issues.

common_issues.name: Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD)
common_issues.early_warning: Back pain, reluctance to jump, hind limb weakness or paralysis

profiles.care_tips: Prevent jumping on/off furniture — use ramps and steps. IVDD surgery can cost $5,000–$10,000; pet insurance is strongly recommended. Keep weight healthy to protect the spine.

common_issues.name: Brachycephalic Syndrome
common_issues.early_warning: Loud snoring, labored breathing, cyanotic gums during exercise

common_issues.name: Eye Proptosis
common_issues.early_warning: Eye bulging forward after trauma — emergency

profiles.care_tips: Keep pugs cool and calm — they can overheat rapidly. Airway surgery is common and often life-changing. Never leave in a hot car or bright sun.

common_issues.name: Hip Dysplasia
common_issues.early_warning: Stiffness when rising, rear limb weakness

common_issues.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy
common_issues.early_warning: Night blindness, hesitancy in dim light

common_issues.name: Von Willebrand Disease
common_issues.early_warning: Prolonged bleeding from cuts, bloody nose

common_issues.name: Ear Infections
common_issues.early_warning: Head shaking, scratching at ears, odor

profiles.care_tips: Floppy ears trap moisture — clean and dry weekly to prevent chronic ear infections. OFA hip testing on parent dogs is important when purchasing a puppy.
Regular ear cleaning is essential with drop ears. Annual wellness checks should include adrenal function assessment in middle age. OFA certified parents are preferred.
